mission: Build a polished, credible, premium political creator platform that helps Democratic and center-left campaigns and organizations find, brief, manage, and activate creators without feeling like a generic influencer marketplace.
primary_goals: Deliver a production-minded milestone 1 with marketing pages, auth, role selection, onboarding, and dashboard shells.
Maintain a premium, high-trust visual direction that feels investor-ready and category-leading.
Own the auth email experience so confirmation, magic link, reset, invite, email change, and reauthentication flows feel app-native.
Preserve durable project knowledge in repo-backed local memory files so future sessions can reconstruct state.
secondary_goals: Improve conversion and trust on the homepage for campaigns and creators.
Keep the repo organized, reusable, and easy to extend in milestone 2.
Document setup and deployment paths clearly for Vercel, Supabase, and email infrastructure.
non_goals: Do not build a generic influencer marketplace.
Do not overbuild dashboard/admin features beyond milestone 1 shells.
Do not add unrelated new features just because they are possible.
Do not rely on Supabase's default shared email sender in production.
hard_rules: Preserve a premium political SaaS direction with more blue than red.
Use Next.js App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Supabase, and Vercel.
Keep secrets server-side only and never commit real secrets to the repo.
Create and maintain migration files for durable schema changes.
Treat project memory as durable local and repo-backed state rather than relying on chat context.
strong_preferences: Reference-led, product-led marketing design rather than generic Tailwind SaaS styling.
Heavy emphasis on TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, Bluesky, and X creator use cases on the homepage.
Readable, branded auth email copy and app-native auth landing routes.
optional_preferences: Use real screenshots/assets when available to replace polished placeholders.
Add custom auth domain support if the infrastructure and plan allow it.
business_constraints: Target buyers are Democratic/progressive campaigns, PACs, committees, issue groups, and aligned organizations.
Target creators are political or persuasion-oriented content creators.
The platform must feel trustworthy, premium, and mission-driven.
technical_constraints: Milestone 1 schema centers on profiles, organizations, creator_profiles, and campaign_profiles.
Supabase SSR clients must work with the App Router.
The auth email hook implementation currently uses Resend and Supabase Edge Functions.
Production deployment assumes Vercel and a hosted Supabase project.
compliance_notes: Do not record real secrets inside project memory files.
Mark inferred facts clearly and avoid storing guesses as confirmed truth.
definitions_of_done: Requested code changes are implemented and verified with typecheck/build where feasible.
Relevant project memory JSON files are updated before the session ends.
Open blockers, risks, and next steps are captured durably.
Live infrastructure status is documented when changes happen outside the repo.
